The SAST scan identified 507 findings across DVWA-2. The overall risk score is 100/100, classified as Critical risk. Security grade: D. Policy status: FAIL.

Affected Components

Code Analysis Architecture

Visualization of scanned codebase modules, file distribution, and vulnerability hotspots.

Methodology & Scoring

Analysis Approach

Static Application Security Testing (SAST) analyzes source code, bytecode, and binary code for security vulnerabilities without executing the application. Pattern matching, taint analysis, and data-flow analysis identify potential weaknesses at the code level.

Severity Classification

  • Critical — CVSS 9.0–10.0
  • High — CVSS 7.0–8.9
  • Medium — CVSS 4.0–6.9
  • Low — CVSS 0.1–3.9

Risk Score Methodology

Risk score (0–100) is computed from weighted severity counts, exploit maturity, EPSS probability, affected surface area, and patch availability. Higher scores indicate greater risk.

Standards & References

  • MITRE CWE — Common Weakness Enumeration
  • OWASP Top 10 — Web Application Security Risks
  • CVSS v3.1 — Common Vulnerability Scoring System
  • EPSS — Exploit Prediction Scoring System

Vulnerability Detail

OAuth state parameter not validated

Impact: Significant impact on confidentiality and integrity.

Technical Explanation

Vulnerable code pattern detected at vulnerabilities/api/source/impossible.php line 10

  • </p> <p> The order system uses <a href='https://oauth.net/2/'>OAuth 2.0</a> for authentication. Being able to automate using this in your tools will greatly help with efficiency, removing the need to manually login and copy access tokens around. Use this level to practice setting up OAuth 2.0 in your testing tool of choice, for me this is <a href='https://www.postman.com/'>Postman</a> which is then proxied through <a href='https://portswigger.net/burp'>Burp</a>, but you can pick whatever tools are most appropriate for your testing environment. </p> <p>
